experiment: Confidence-Cascade Memory - Gate #2 (does the cascade dominate?)
regime: small native window (native 6 / buffer 50 turns), abstention-gated cascade
pod_notes: 2 pods failed on flaky hosts (3090 crash-loop, 4090 no-start) before pod #3 (3090) served; cost includes the wasted pods
dataset: gate2_main.jsonl - 30/cell, 600 plant items, seed0=500000; dist {5,20,40,60,80} x {single_fact,distractor_dense} x {easy,hard}
operating_point: theta=-0.1 (logprob gate is INERT here; cascade is effectively abstention-gated -> single point)

(i)_B4_dominates_B1_and_B3.note: positive delta = cascade MORE expensive; needed < 0. Cheap-first loses even to random at equal budget.

verdict: NO-GO for the novelty claim (honest negative). Do not proceed to Gate #3.
findings: F1: logprob confidence sensor collapses under verbalized abstention (mean_logprob ~ -0.003 for 'I don't know'); regime-fragile, does not transfer from Gate #1's full-window regime.

F2: always-RAG dominates on cost; a cost cascade needs cheap rungs with a high hit rate. Under uniform fact-distance, buffer-scoped cheap rungs fail on 60% of facts and the cascade pays for them before reaching full-RAG.
